zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Joann Trott於1482年出生在英國。她於1506年與William Trott在英國英格蘭薩默塞特斯塔普萊格洛福結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特奧特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Trott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Somerset and Devon): in southwestern England, usually a variant of   Trout  .
South German: metonymic variant of   Trotter   2.

English (Somerset and Devon): nickname, either for one who moves quickly, at the pace of a trotting horse, from Middle English trot(te) (Old French trot) ‘trot’, perhaps given to a messenger (see Trotter , Trotman ); or occasionally perhaps from Middle English trot(te), trat(te) ‘old woman, hag’ (Anglo-Norman French trote).

variants.name: Trout
meanings: English (mainly Devon): nickname from Middle English trout(e), trut(e), trought(e), trot ‘trout’ (Old English trūht), perhaps for someone who caught or sold trout, or who was thought to resemble the fish in some way.

nameMeanings.name: Joann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old French Jo(h)anne, and so a doublet of Joan . This too was revived as a given name in its own right in the first half of the 20th century. It has to some extent been influenced by the independently formed combination Jo Anne.

variants.name: Johanne
meanings: and Norwegian feminine form of   Johan .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Joann Trott was born in 1482, in England. She married William Trott in 1506, in Staplegrove, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 6 daughters. She was buried in Otterford, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

ko.St John's Church, Skilgate, Somerset, En: 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 스킬가트 스트 존'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Joann Trott(은)는 1482년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1506년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 스타플레그로브에서 William Trott(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 오터퍼드에 묻혔다.
